steps.title: Read the shaded region from the figure
steps.content: From the figure, the shaded region is bounded by one or more lines and occupies a specific portion of the coordinate plane. To find which ordered pair is a solution, plot each candidate point on the same axes and check whether it lies inside the shaded region (including any solid boundary) or outside it.

steps.title: Test each choice against the shaded region
steps.content:
Locate each candidate point on the graph and compare its position to the shaded region shown:
  • Choice A - $(-5, 0)$: lies on the negative $x$-axis, outside the shaded region in the figure. Incorrect
  • Choice B - $(0, 5)$: lies on the positive $y$-axis, outside the shaded region in the figure. Incorrect
  • Choice C - $(0, -5)$: lies on the negative $y$-axis, outside the shaded region in the figure. Incorrect
  • Choice D - $(5, 0)$: lies on the positive $x$-axis, inside the shaded region shown in the figure. Correct
Only $(5, 0)$ falls within the shaded region, so it is the only ordered pair that satisfies the inequality.
